brief: Episode packaged into a publish-ready summary with three clip moments around tiny binaries, artifact contracts, and GitHub as the control plane.

outputNotes.show notes: Show notes are the quickest publishable output from the episode.
outputNotes.clip list: Clips matter because they let one episode become many short-form entry points.
outputNotes.article draft: The article draft shows how the same episode can repurpose into long-form copy.
outputNotes.RSS-ready bundle: The bundle is the distribution-ready version of the same episode artifact.

searchSummary: Tiny binaries, artifact contracts, GitHub control plane.
whyItMatters: Episode search shows how multiple recordings can reuse the same repurposing path while still surfacing the right moments and outputs for each one.
searchIntro: Search across the seeded episode set to see how the same pipeline handles different topics and still yields clips, notes, and publish-ready outputs.

brief: Episode focused on artifact-native CMS design, local-first outputs, and why prompts should not be the user interface.
searchSummary: Artifact-native CMS, local-first outputs, no prompt UI.
whyItMatters: Once there are multiple episodes, search and compression stop being abstract. They become the way users revisit the right conversation quickly.

brief: Episode covered demo fleets, productized artifacts, and how one source recording should generate notes, clips, and release surfaces without branching into separate tools.
searchSummary: Demo fleets, productized artifacts, one source to many surfaces.
whyItMatters: A multi-episode dataset makes reuse visible. The user can search related episodes instead of staring at one isolated output file.
